steps.title: Set the shifted function equal to zero
steps.content: The $x$-intercept of the graph of $y=f(x)+5$ occurs when $y=0$. So solve $$f(x)+5=0.$$ Since $f(x)=4d(24x+25)+15$, this becomes $$4d(24x+25)+20=0.$$

steps.title: Solve for the x-coordinate
steps.content: Expand and isolate $x$: $$96dx+100d+20=0$$ $$96dx=-100d-20$$ $$x=\frac{-100d-20}{96d}.$$ Therefore the intercept is $$\left(\frac{-100d-20}{96d},0\right).$$

steps.title: Check with a sample value
steps.content: For a quick check, test an easy value such as $d=1$. The formula gives $$x=\frac{-100(1)-20}{96(1)}=\frac{-120}{96}.$$ Substituting this into $4(24x+25)+20$ gives $0$, so it is the correct intercept.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A — $\left(\frac{-100d+20}{96d},0\right)$ has the wrong sign on $20$. Choice C — $\left(5-\frac{100d+15}{96d},0\right)$ treats the vertical shift like a horizontal change. Choice D — $\left(-\frac{40}{96d+5},0\right)$ incorrectly puts the added $5$ in the denominator. Only Choice B — $\left(\frac{-100d-20}{96d},0\right)$ matches the intercept.
